notes: Reproduced DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp with SkiaSharp 2.88.3 on Blazor WASM (net8.0, SDK 8.0.417). The root cause is that SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly 2.88.3 only ships emscripten 2.0.23 static libraries (.a files) for .NET 6. When targeting .NET 7+ (which requires emscripten 3.1.12+), the NativeFileReference glob finds no matching .a files, so libSkiaSharp is not linked into dotnet.native.wasm. Fixed in SkiaSharp 3.119.2 which ships emscripten 3.1.34 .a files for .NET 8. Tested on net8.0 instead of reporter's net7.0 (no .NET 7 SDK available) — same root cause applies.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project targeting net8.0 with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.3 and WasmBuildNative=true
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 2.88.3
reproductionSteps.output: Restored Repro.csproj. PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor' version '2.88.3' added.

filesCreated.description: Blazor WASM project file with WasmBuildNative=true and SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.3
Razor page reproducing the reporter's SkiaSharp code — creates SKSurface and draws text

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor WASM project with native WASM compilation — build succeeds but wasm-ld does NOT link any libSkiaSharp.a
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded. 0 Warning(s) 0 Error(s). CRITICAL: wasm-ld command linked only system .a files (libicudata, libmono, etc.) — no libSkiaSharp.a was included. NativeAssets.WebAssembly 2.88.3 has no emscripten 3.1.34 assets for net8.0.

reproductionSteps.description: Serve app with dotnet run, navigate browser to localhost:5111, click Create button — DllNotFoundException thrown in browser at runtime
reproductionSteps.output: Browser console: ERROR: System.TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo' threw an exception. ---> System.DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp at SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo..cctor() --- End of inner exception stack trace --- at Repro.Pages.Home.Create(). Page status text showed same exception.

versionResults.notes: DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp at runtime. NativeAssets.WebAssembly 2.88.3 only ships emscripten 2.0.23 .a files (for .NET 6). No .a files matched for net8.0 (emscripten 3.1.34), so libSkiaSharp was not linked into dotnet.native.wasm.

SkiaSharp rendered text successfully. NativeAssets.WebAssembly 3.119.2 ships emscripten 3.1.34 .a files which were correctly linked into dotnet.native.wasm. Browser console showed 'SUCCESS: SkiaSharp rendered text' and page status was 'End'.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the detailed report and repro projects!

We were able to reproduce the `DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp` with SkiaSharp **2.88.3** on Blazor WebAssembly.

The root cause is that SkiaSharp 2.88.3 was released before .NET 7 and only included the WASM native libraries (`.a` files) for the .NET 6 Emscripten toolchain (v2.0.23). When targeting .NET 7+, the build system couldn't find matching native assets, so `libSkiaSharp` was never linked into `dotnet.native.wasm`.

The good news is that this was fixed in subsequent releases. Upgrading resolves the issue.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 2.88.3 | ❌ `DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp` |
| 3.119.2 | ✅ Works correctly |

**Workaround:** Upgrade SkiaSharp to **2.88.6+** (which added .NET 7 WASM support) or **3.x** (which has full .NET 8+ WASM support with SIMD).

actionability.reason: DllNotFoundException reproduced with SkiaSharp 2.88.3 which lacked .NET 7+ WASM native assets. Fixed in SkiaSharp 3.119.2 which includes emscripten 3.1.34 static libraries for .NET 8. The fix was shipped in subsequent releases after PR #2254.

workarounds: Upgrade SkiaSharp to 2.88.6+ which includes .NET 7 WASM static libraries
Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x on .NET 8+ for full WASM support with SIMD and threading options

actions.description: Apply bug, Blazor, and WASM labels
Close as fixed — upgrading to SkiaSharp 2.88.6+ or 3.x resolves the issue
